SEARCH
Package io.stimulustech.filequeue.processor

Class QueueProcessor<T extends FileQueueItem>

java.lang.Object
io.stimulustech.filequeue.processor.QueueProcessor<T>
Type Parameters:
T - the type of FileQueueItem managed by this processor
public class QueueProcessor<T extends FileQueueItem> extends Object
Internal queue processor responsible for dispatching FileQueueItem instances to a Consumer, persisting items that cannot be immediately processed, and retrying them according to the configured delay strategy.

Items are first offered directly to the thread pool supplied via QueueProcessorBuilder. When no thread is available the item is serialised to an H2 MVStore database on disk. A background cleaner task periodically polls the database, deserialises each item, and re-submits it for processing once the configured retry delay has elapsed.

Retry behaviour is controlled by QueueProcessor.RetryDelayAlgorithm: FIXED applies a constant interval, while EXPONENTIAL doubles the delay on each attempt up to maxRetryDelay. Once an item exceeds maxTries the optional Expiration handler is called and the item is discarded.

This class is for internal use only. Use FileQueue as the public entry point.

Author:
Valentin Popov, Jamie Band

    Description
    static enum 
    QueueProcessor.RetryDelayAlgorithm
    Algorithm used to calculate the delay between successive delivery attempts.

  • Method Details

    • destroy

      public static void destroy()
      Shuts down the shared MVStore cleanup scheduler and awaits termination for up to 60 seconds. Call this once, at application exit, to cleanly release background threads.

    • submit

      public void submit(T item, int acquireWait, TimeUnit acquireWaitUnit) throws IOException, InterruptedException
      Submits an item for immediate processing. If the thread pool cannot accept the item within the specified wait time, an IllegalStateException is thrown. If the pool is busy the item is persisted to disk and retried later.
      Parameters:
      item - queue item to process
      acquireWait - maximum time to wait for a free slot
      acquireWaitUnit - time unit for acquireWait
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the queue is not running or the slot could not be acquired within the wait period
      InterruptedException - if the calling thread is interrupted while waiting
      IOException - if the item cannot be serialised to the persistent store

    • submit

      public void submit(T item) throws IllegalStateException, IOException, InterruptedException
      Submits an item for processing, blocking indefinitely until a free slot becomes available. If the thread pool is busy, the item is persisted to disk and retried later.
      Parameters:
      item - queue item to process
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the queue is not running
      IOException - if the item could not be serialized
      InterruptedException - if the current thread is interrupted

    • close

      public void close()
      Stops the queue processor and waits for all in-flight items to finish processing.

      Shutdown sequence:

      1. Set doRun = false — no new submissions will be accepted.
      2. Release at least one extra permit (and up to maxQueueSize extra permits) so any threads blocked in submit(T, int, java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit) can acquire, re-check doRun, and exit cleanly with IllegalStateException.
      3. Cancel the background cleanup scheduler (without interrupting the shared scheduler thread, so in-flight cleanup runs for other queues are unaffected).
      4. Acquire cleanerLock to wait for any currently-executing QueueProcessor<T extends FileQueueItem>.processor.io.stimulustech.filequeue.QueueProcessor.MVStoreCleaner run to finish — this guarantees the MVStore is still open for the cleaner's entire execution before we close it.
      5. Wait on the Phaser for every dispatched QueueProcessor<T extends FileQueueItem>.processor.io.stimulustech.filequeue.QueueProcessor.ProcessItem to complete its run() method — including any push-back to disk.
      6. Close the underlying MVStore (safe because all push-back is already done).

      This method is idempotent; a second call is a no-op.

      Executor lifecycle: the ExecutorService supplied at construction is not shut down by this method — the caller owns it and is responsible for shutting it down after this method returns. The executor must not be shut down while the queue is running; doing so causes rejected tasks to be pushed to disk rather than executed.

    • setMaxQueueSize

      public void setMaxQueueSize(int maxQueueSize)
      Adjusts the maximum number of items that may be held concurrently by the queue.
      Parameters:
      maxQueueSize - new maximum queue size; must be >= 0
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if maxQueueSize is negative

    • size

      public long size()
      Returns the number of items currently persisted in the on-disk queue store.
      Returns:
      number of items in the persistent store

    • getQueueBaseDir

      public Path getQueueBaseDir()
      Returns the base directory used by the underlying MVStore queue.
      Returns:
      path to the queue database directory

    • reopen

      public void reopen() throws IllegalStateException
      Reopens the underlying MVStore queue after it has been closed.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the store cannot be reopened

    • availablePermits

      public int availablePermits()
      Returns the number of permits currently available, i.e. the number of additional items that may be submitted without blocking.
      Returns:
      number of available queue slots

    • getQueuePath

      public Path getQueuePath()
      Returns the queue database path.
      Returns:
      queue database path
